---
title: "Attack-Centric Framework (ACF)"
description: "A defensive framework that re-orients cybersecurity strategy around attacker behavior rather than compliance checklists."
author: "Kader Mohideen"
date: "2024-06-13"
categories: [Cybersecurity, Framework, Zero Trust]
---

## Summary

The **Attack-Centric Framework (ACF)** is an ongoing exploration of how organizations can re-orient their defensive posture around how attackers actually operate, rather than around audit and compliance checklists.

## Components

1. Unified risk assessment
2. Proactive security (zero trust + threat intel)
3. Dynamic defense protocols
4. Compliance integration (GDPR, ISO/IEC 27001)
5. Threat-centric analytics
6. Industry-specific customization
7. Continuous improvement
8. Security-conscious culture

## Status

Ongoing — write-ups continue on the [blog](../../blog.qmd):

- [ACF — AC1: Introduction](../../blog/2024-06-13-acf-ac1/)
- [ACF — AC2: Eight key components](../../blog/2024-06-13-acf-ac2/)
